answer:
The Pinterest API uses OAuth 2.0 for authentication [1][2]. The primary flow involves making an authorization request to the Pinterest OAuth page, handling the redirect to exchange an authorization code for an access token, and storing that token for subsequent API calls [1]. Access tokens should be refreshed as needed using a refresh token [1][2].

For testing purposes, you may use product-limited tokens, which are available upon approval for trial access and expire after 24 hours [1]. You can also use the Token Debugger tool to verify token scopes [3].

Example using the Python SDK to configure an access token:

```python
configuration = openapi_generated.pinterest_client.Configuration(
    host = "https://api.pinterest.com/v5"
)
configuration.access_token = 'YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN'
```

More details on scopes and the full authentication process are available in the official Pinterest Developer documentation [1][3][4].
